steps.title: Identify the clause boundary
steps.content: Before the blank is an independent clause: “polymers have been engineered to repair themselves.” After comes a participial phrase (“aided by embedded microcapsules”) followed by a second independent clause: “such materials begin to self-heal.”

steps.title: Apply the rule for separating independent clauses
steps.content: Choice C uses a period to properly separate the two independent clauses. The other options use commas or dashes instead, failing to mark the boundary where a new independent clause begins and thus creating comma splices or run-on sentences. That matches choice C — C is correct.
